title: Human Resources Management Course
description: The "Comprehensive Human Resource Management" course is specifically designed for business owners, CEOs, middle and senior managers of small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) in Vietnam. The course provides comprehensive knowledge, skills, tools, and practical strategies for effective human resource management, cost optimization, and building a strong workforce. Students will be guided step-by-step from developing a human resource strategy aligned with business objectives to implementing modern tools such as HR management software, data applications, and process automation to improve business performance.

full_summary:
## Course Introduction

The "Human Resource Management" course provides a comprehensive foundation in modern HR management, focusing on putting people at the center of all business activities. The course is structured into 8 in-depth modules, each focusing on a key area such as organizational structure, recruitment, training, compensation, corporate culture, and technology application. The target audience includes business owners, CEOs, middle/senior managers, and HR departments at small and medium-sized enterprises, aiming to help learners confidently build effective HR systems, save costs, increase team cohesion, and optimize business performance. The course stands out by updating practical methods, modern tools, systematic processes, and realistic exercises, enabling learners to easily apply them in the Vietnamese business environment.

---

## Detailed Content by Chapter

### Course Overview

The opening chapter helps learners grasp an overview of the course, identify the role of HR strategy in business development, and understand the importance of putting people at the core. The course consists of 8 modules, each focusing on a key area: organizational structure design, comprehensive recruitment, training and development, compensation & benefits, corporate culture, HR technology application (other modules not listed). The goal is to equip learners with up-to-date knowledge and practical methods, enabling them to confidently manage HR, build an engaged work environment, and contribute to business success. By the end of this chapter, learners are ready to enter the first module on organizational structure design.

---

### Conclusion

The concluding chapter summarizes the entire course content, emphasizing the 8 foundational modules: strategy and modern trends, organizational structure and HR strategy design, comprehensive recruitment process, workforce training and development, performance evaluation, compensation & benefits, corporate culture, technology application, and automation. Each module includes practical exercises to reinforce knowledge. After the course, learners will have the foundation to build a strong, modern HR system for their business and are encouraged to download and complete the exercises to maximize learning effectiveness.

---

### Module 1: Strategy and Modern Trends in Human Resource Management

This module helps learners understand the strategic role of HR management in modern businesses, distinguishing between traditional HR (focused only on administration) and modern HR (strategic partner, driving sustainable development). Learners are introduced to digital transformation, globalization, flexible work, HR 4.0 technology applications (Base.vn, Lark, MISA HRM…), online recruitment, HR Analytics (HR data analysis), and HR development levels in businesses (administrative, professional, strategic partner). The chapter also emphasizes the collaboration between HR, CEO, and middle management, HR department models for SMEs, recruitment processes, compensation management, training, building corporate culture, and notes when applying lean HR models. Practical examples of technology application, online recruitment, and exercises help learners better visualize real-world implementation.

---

### Module 2: Organizational Structure Design and HR Strategy

This module focuses on selecting and building an organizational structure model that fits the business’s goals, scale, strategy, and culture. Learners are introduced to popular models: functional, product/project-based, matrix, flat; analysis of pros and cons, and a step-by-step guide to designing an organizational structure from goal setting to piloting and adjustment. The HR strategy section emphasizes alignment with business strategy, steps to build an effective HR strategy (goal setting, current state assessment, planning, implementation, measurement, adjustment), and measurement tools such as KPI, OKR, Balanced Scorecard. Learners are also introduced to the Agile model in HR management (Scrum, Kanban), conditions for implementing Agile HR, and practical examples such as organizational structure at Thế Giới Di Động, tech startups, and Agile application in recruitment. Key highlights include flexibility, technology application, cross-functional collaboration, and building an empowering culture.

---

### Module 3: Comprehensive Recruitment in the Digital Age

This module guides learners through a 7-step recruitment process: identifying needs, job description, posting/promoting, CV screening, interviews (multiple rounds, STAR technique, competency and situational assessment), offer – completing procedures, onboarding handover. Learners learn how to build an employer brand (core values, benefits, development opportunities, communication), employer brand building process (defining EVP, storytelling, leveraging internal channels, measurement and adjustment), and a 4-stage onboarding process (pre-start, first day, first week–first month, after 1–3 months). The module emphasizes objective, transparent assessment, clear role assignment in onboarding, and creating a positive experience for candidates and new employees. Practical examples of recruitment, employer branding, and onboarding help learners easily apply these concepts.

---

### Module 4: Training and Workforce Development

This module helps learners understand the role of internal training systems in enhancing capabilities, saving costs, and retaining employees. The internal training building process includes: needs assessment, planning, program design, evaluation, continuous improvement. Learners are introduced to methods for motivating learning (learning culture, rewards, diverse formats, linking with career paths). The succession leadership development section presents a 5-stage process: needs identification, identifying and assessing potential leaders, designing development paths, practice – monitoring, evaluation – appointment. Learners are guided on building a leadership competency framework, using mentoring, coaching, job rotation, challenging assignments, budgeting, and measuring effectiveness. Practical examples help learners clearly visualize the succession leadership development process.

---

### Module 5: Performance Evaluation and Achievement Management

This module equips learners with knowledge of performance evaluation systems (KPI, OKR), the process of building a performance management system from setting strategic goals to linking with rewards and benefits. Learners gain skills in fair, objective evaluation, avoiding bias (Halo/Horn effect, central tendency, etc.), transparent evaluation processes, using 360-degree feedback, quick interviews, and self-assessment. The rewards and benefits section guides how to set up a bonus fund, link with KPI/OKR, build specific bonus scales, communicate policies clearly, publicize results, distinguish between short-term and long-term rewards, and regularly review and adjust. Practical examples of building KPI/OKR, employee evaluation, and rewards/benefits help learners apply these concepts in real situations.

---

### Module 6: Fair Compensation and Benefits

This module helps learners understand the role of competitive compensation structures, steps to build a compensation structure (market survey, positioning, choosing a pay strategy, defining pay bands, implementation – monitoring), compensation components (base salary, allowances, bonuses, commissions, stock options), how to arrange compensation structures (fixed – variable income, pay grades/bands), and notes on implementation. The non-financial benefits section covers importance, popular benefit programs (healthcare, flexible benefits, happy work environment), balancing costs and benefits, legal – tax – insurance considerations. Practical examples of compensation structures and non-financial benefits help learners build policies suitable for their business.

---

### Module 7: Corporate Culture and Employee Engagement

This module helps learners understand the concept and role of corporate culture, core values, mission, vision, and how to build, communicate, and implement culture in management practices. Learners are guided on integrating cultural values into recruitment, training, evaluation, rewards, internal communication; identifying challenges and solutions in culture building. The employee engagement activities section covers the role, popular types of activities (teambuilding, internal culture, charity, knowledge sharing, activities for employees’ families), process for organizing engagement activities, and notes for implementation. Practical examples of corporate culture (Google, Netflix, Alibaba…) and engagement activities help learners apply these effectively in their businesses.

---

### Module 8: Technology Application, Automation, and HR Optimization

This module provides knowledge on HRM software solutions (profile management, timekeeping – payroll, recruitment – onboarding, training, performance management, benefits), the process of selecting and implementing HRM, and the benefits of application. Learners learn how to apply data and HR analytics through steps: data collection – consolidation, cleaning, choosing analysis tools, problem analysis – conclusion, using common metrics (turnover rate, recruitment cost, training effectiveness, eNPS…). The automation process section presents benefits, commonly automated areas, implementation process, maximizing software features, and security – legal notes. Practical examples of HRM software application, HR data analysis, and process automation help learners optimize time and enhance HR management effectiveness.

---

## Core Knowledge & Skills

- **Building HR strategies aligned with business goals:** Ability to analyze, identify HR needs, plan recruitment, training, development, and retention of talent in sync with business strategy.
- **Flexible organizational structure design:** Select, build, and adjust organizational structure models (functional, product, matrix, flat) suitable for business scale, goals, and culture.
- **Modern recruitment process:** Apply the 7-step recruitment process, use STAR interview techniques, build employer branding, and implement systematic onboarding to help new employees integrate quickly.
- **Internal training system & succession leadership development:** Build training processes from needs assessment to evaluation and maintenance; develop a succession leadership team through mentoring, coaching, job rotation, and challenging assignments.
- **Performance evaluation & achievement management:** Design transparent, fair KPI and OKR systems; implement objective evaluation processes, 360-degree feedback, and link evaluation with rewards/benefits.
- **Compensation & benefits structure building:** Conduct market surveys, define pay strategies, design competitive compensation systems, and build appropriate financial and non-financial benefit programs.
- **Building & maintaining corporate culture:** Define mission, vision, core values; communicate and implement these values into management practices; organize effective team engagement activities.
- **HR technology application & automation:** Select and implement HRM software, apply HR Analytics, automate HR processes, and leverage data for accurate decision-making, optimizing time and resources.

---

## Learning Outcomes

After the course, learners will be able to:

- Build and adjust organizational structures suitable for business goals, scale, and strategy.
- Design and implement HR strategies aligned with business strategy, synchronizing objectives and resources.
- Apply the 7-step recruitment process, build employer branding, and effectively implement onboarding for new employees.
- Build and operate internal training systems, develop succession leadership teams, and foster continuous learning motivation within the business.
- Design performance evaluation systems (KPI, OKR), organize fair and objective evaluations, and link with rewards and benefits.
- Build competitive compensation structures and financial and non-financial benefit policies suitable for business resources.
- Build, communicate, and maintain corporate culture, organize team engagement activities, and enhance morale and work effectiveness.
- Apply HRM software, leverage HR data (HR Analytics), automate HR management processes, and optimize time and efficiency for leaders and the HR department.
- Evaluate, adjust, and continuously improve the HR system based on real data and internal feedback.
- Confidently implement modern HR management tools, models, and processes in small and medium-sized enterprises in Vietnam.

---

## Who Should Take This Course

This course is suitable for:

- Business owners, CEOs, middle and senior managers of small and medium-sized enterprises.
- HR managers/deputy managers, HR specialists, department managers seeking to build or optimize HR systems.
- Those wishing to update their knowledge, skills, and tools in modern, practical HR management.
- No specific educational background or HR management experience is required (not mentioned in the lectures).
